description:
You are acting as a build-engineer who has just received a JSON
  manifest describing the three binary artifacts that will ship in the
  next release.  Your goal is to make sure that the manifest is valid
  and then generate two very small helper files that other automated
  tools expect.

  1.  Location of the working directory  
      /home/user/build

  2.  Files that already exist inside that directory  
      • /home/user/build/artifacts.json  
      • /home/user/build/artifact_schema.json  

     Both of these files are already present when the session starts;
     do NOT rename or delete them.

  3.  Validation step  
      a)  Use any standard command-line JSON-Schema validator you
          prefer (for example `ajv`, `jsonschema`, `pykwalify`,
          etc.) to check that artifacts.json conforms to
          artifact_schema.json.  
      b)  The outcome must be recorded in a plain-text log file called  
          /home/user/build/build_validation.log  
          The file must contain exactly three lines – one per artifact
          – in the following fixed format (punctuation, capitalisation
          and spacing must match exactly; no blank lines at the end):

              libwidget: 1.2.3 - OK
              libgizmo: 4.5.6 - OK
              app-thing: 0.9.1 - OK

          If the schema validation fails you would normally write
          “- FAIL” on the relevant line, but the automated grader will
          only look for the “- OK” version above, so make sure the
          JSON validates before you write the file.

  4.  jq extraction step  
      After successful validation, use `jq` to extract just the
      “name” field from every artifact and write them – one per line,
      no trailing whitespace, Unix line-endings – to

          /home/user/build/artifact_names.txt

      Expected final content:

          libwidget
          libgizmo
          app-thing

  5.  Summary of artefacts that must exist at the end  
      • /home/user/build/build_validation.log (exactly 3 lines, exact
        wording and order shown above)  
      • /home/user/build/artifact_names.txt (exactly 3 lines, listed in
        the same order as in artifacts.json)

  You may use at most a handful of shell commands (roughly three or
  four) to finish the task.
name: task_000000_a7b7881b
